title: Connection refused due to blocked response
symptom: When trying to access the n8n instance from an external link, the browser shows 'n8n.domain.com refused to connect. ERR_BLOCKED_BY_RESPONSE'. This prevents OAuth2 authorization from completing.
not_this_if: Accessing from within the same network
root_cause: The error occurs due to restrictive security headers in the Traefik configuration, particularly related to cross-origin policies. These headers prevent the browser from establishing a connection to the n8n instance from an external source.
fix_steps: Update the Traefik dynamic configuration to include the necessary headers for cross-origin access.

Add the following lines to your n8n-headers middleware: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: 'unsafe-none', Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: 'cross-origin', X-Frame-Options: 'ALLOWALL', Content-Security-Policy: 'frame-ancestors *'.
Restart the Traefik service to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Modify the Traefik configuration to adjust the cross-origin headers and restart the service.
